chief_complaint: A patient presented with severe acidosis and normal blood glucose levels following a traumatic brain injury.
history: The patient was taking Sitagliptin, a DPP-4 inhibitor, and had a history of traumatic brain injury.
exam: Severe acidosis (pH 7.16), low bicarbonate, and elevated anion gap
labs: Low bicarbonate (2.8 mmol/L), elevated anion gap (24.9 mmol/L), and urinary ketones
